page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-192-highlssd-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores running at 4.0 GHz. The x86_64 architecture features dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled, supported by 1.5 TB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance includes a massive local storage capacity of 77,304 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, with no integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers exceptional read-only bandwidth, reaching 13,059.77 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 462.85 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, reflecting high throughput capabilities. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with single-core stress-ng scores in the middle 50% and single-threaded gzip compression falling in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal server offers high resource density, combining massive local NVMe storage with a substantial memory footprint. This profile reduces the need for external storage attachments, optimizing data-transfer costs for storage-heavy workloads. The primary tradeoff lies in single-threaded execution efficiency, where the system performs at average or below-average levels despite its high multi-core throughput and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX.

This server is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput Redis caching layers, and intensive data-processing pipelines. The combination of 77.3 TB of local NVMe SSD storage and 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM makes it an optimal fit for distributed databases, real-time analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications requiring direct bare-metal hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-192-highlssd-metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs (96 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores at 4.0 GHz) and 1.5 TB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it integrates a massive 77,304 GB of local NVMe SSD storage but lacks GPU acceleration. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read bandwidth of up to 13,059.77 GB/sec, and superior database and Redis processing speeds. While single-threaded performance is comparatively moderate, its high resource density and direct hardware access provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy deployments. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput caching layers, and intensive enterprise data-processing workloads.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive local NVMe storage with high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive database and data-processing workloads.

1.5 TB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 system memory
77,304 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results

Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and data-intensive workloads
